ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Hoi, Emissary of Flame.' The small robed alien figure in elaborate ceremonial garb with flame energy effects matches Hoi's canonical design: a diminutive non-human sorcerer whose power is manipulation and ceremony, not physical combat. Character name is in the title.

trivia_bullets.text: Hoi is the secondary antagonist of Dragon Ball Z: Wrath of the Dragon and the last surviving member of the Kashvar — an alien race of evil sorcerers who believe themselves superior to all other species. His stated goal is to use Hirudegarn to 'cleanse' the universe of all non-Kashvars [Villains Wiki: Hoi, WebSearch: Hoi Kashvar villain role].
sources: WebSearch: Hoi Dragon Ball villain Kashvar Wrath of the Dragon

trivia_bullets.text: Hoi's scheme involves sustained deception: he manipulates the Z-Fighters into opening Tapion's Music Box by fabricating a cover story, presenting himself as a desperate victim seeking the heroes' help. His manipulation of Bulma and the Z cast into inadvertently freeing Tapion — and thereby eventually freeing Hirudegarn — is his primary narrative function. He is not a fighter; he uses others as instruments [Villains Wiki: Hoi, Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Wrath of the Dragon].
sources: Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Wrath of the Dragon

trivia_bullets.text: Hoi dies by irony: after engineering Hirudegarn's full resurrection, he is killed by the monster itself — inadvertently crushed underfoot — ending the Kashvar bloodline entirely. This eliminates the only entity capable of controlling Hirudegarn, making the monster's defeat by Goku all the more final [Villains Wiki: Hoi, WebSearch: Hoi death Hirudegarn crushes].
sources: WebSearch: Hoi Dragon Ball villain Kashvar Wrath of the Dragon
